text:
Robust citywide traffic flow prediction is a cornerstone of intelligent transportation systems. Despite significant progress in modeling spatio-temporal dependencies, current state-of-the-art methods face two major hurdles: (i) spatial heterogeneity, where diverse urban regions exhibit unique and often skewed traffic distributions that uniform models fail to capture, and (ii) temporal heterogeneity, where time-varying traffic patterns are often constrained by a shared parameter space that cannot account for the dynamic nature of urban rhythms.

To address these limitations, we introduce Spatio-Temporal Self-Supervised Learning (ST-SSL), a novel framework designed to generate traffic representations that are inherently sensitive to both spatial and temporal diversity. Built upon an integrated architecture of temporal and spatial convolutions, ST-SSL utilizes auxiliary self-supervised learning paradigms to refine its predictive accuracy. The framework employs an adaptive augmentation strategy at both the attribute and structural levels of the traffic flow graph. On top of this augmented data, we construct two specialized SSL tasks that empower the model to differentiate between heterogeneous region-wise functionalities and time-specific dynamics. Extensive evaluations on four benchmark datasets show that ST-SSL consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art baselines. Given the ubiquity of spatio-temporal heterogeneity in urban data, our framework provides a robust template for enhancing various other spatial-temporal applications. Implementation details and code are available at https://github.com/Echo-Ji/ST-SSL.
